title: Energy Freedom Act
text: This act prohibits state and local governments from restricting the use of natural gas or other fossil fuel energy sources in buildings and appliances. No government entity shall adopt or enforce any regulation that bans the use of natural gas, propane, or other fossil fuel energy sources for residential or commercial heating, cooking, or other building uses. Any such regulation is hereby declared void and unenforceable. The legislature finds that consumer choice in energy sources promotes competition, innovation, and affordable energy for all citizens.

title: Voter ID Act
text: Every person desiring to vote shall present photographic identification before receiving a ballot. Acceptable forms of identification include a driver license, military identification card, passport, or government-issued photo identification. Voters who do not present acceptable identification shall be permitted to cast a provisional ballot. Provisional ballots shall only be counted if the voter returns with acceptable identification within the prescribed time period. The state shall provide free identification to any eligible voter who lacks acceptable photo identification.

title: Right to Work Act
text: No person shall be required as a condition of employment or continuation of employment to pay dues, fees, assessments, or other charges of any kind or amount to any labor organization. Any agreement, understanding, or practice that requires membership in or payment to a labor organization as a condition of employment is hereby declared to be against public policy and void. It shall be unlawful for any employer or labor organization to deny employment to any person or to discriminate against any employee by reason of membership or non-membership in any labor union.

title: Lawsuit Abuse Reduction Act
text: This act limits punitive damages in civil actions to the greater of two times compensatory damages or two hundred fifty thousand dollars. No award of punitive damages shall exceed these limits unless the finder of fact determines by clear and convincing evidence that the defendant acted with specific intent to harm the plaintiff. Joint and several liability is hereby abolished in all civil actions. Each defendant shall be liable only for damages proportionate to that defendant's percentage of fault as determined by the trier of fact.

title: Education Savings Account Act
text: Parents of eligible students may apply to receive education savings account funds equal to ninety percent of the per-pupil funding that would otherwise be allocated to the student's assigned public school. Education savings account funds may be used for tuition and fees at private schools, tutoring services, curriculum and textbooks, online learning programs, educational therapies, and other qualifying educational expenses. Participating students are not required to take state standardized assessments. The state shall establish procedures for auditing education savings account expenditures to ensure funds are used for qualifying educational expenses.

title: Regulatory Freeze and Review Act
text: Upon taking office, the governor shall immediately impose a freeze on all pending regulations that have not yet taken effect. No agency shall issue any new regulation without prior approval from the legislature. All existing regulations shall be subject to comprehensive review to determine whether they are necessary, cost-effective, and supported by current scientific and economic evidence. Regulations that fail to demonstrate net economic benefit shall be repealed. Agencies must identify and repeal one existing regulation for every new regulation proposed.

title: Firearm Preemption Act
text: The state legislature hereby occupies and preempts the entire field of legislation touching on firearms, ammunition, and firearm accessories to the complete exclusion of all local ordinances, regulations, or other enactments. No county, city, town, or other political subdivision shall enact any ordinance, regulation, or rule regulating the purchase, sale, transfer, ownership, use, keeping, possession, bearing, transportation, licensing, or registration of firearms or ammunition. Any such local ordinance or regulation is hereby declared null and void.

title: Public Employee Pension Reform Act
text: All newly hired public employees shall be enrolled in a defined contribution retirement plan rather than a defined benefit pension plan. Existing employees may voluntarily transfer their accrued pension benefits to a defined contribution plan. The state shall contribute a matching amount not to exceed six percent of the employee's salary to the defined contribution plan. Defined benefit plans shall be closed to new enrollment. The actuarial unfunded liability of existing defined benefit plans shall be amortized over a period not to exceed twenty years.

title: Telemedicine Modernization Act
text: A healthcare provider may provide telemedicine services to a patient located in this state if the provider holds a valid license to practice in this state or holds a license in another state and provides services consistent with the interstate medical licensure compact. Insurers shall reimburse covered telemedicine services at the same rate as equivalent in-person services. No insurer shall impose geographic restrictions on the availability of telemedicine coverage. A valid physician-patient relationship may be established through telemedicine without a prior in-person visit.

title: Environmental Regulations Preemption Act
text: No state agency shall adopt or enforce any environmental regulation that is more stringent than the corresponding federal regulation without express approval from the legislature. Local governments are hereby preempted from enacting environmental regulations that exceed state or federal standards. The legislature finds that uniform environmental standards promote economic competitiveness and reduce regulatory burden on businesses operating in multiple jurisdictions. Any state agency rule that exceeds federal environmental standards shall be subject to legislative review and approval before taking effect.

title: Occupational Licensing Reform Act
text: No occupational license shall be required unless the legislature finds by clear and convincing evidence that unlicensed practice of the occupation presents a significant risk of harm to the public. All existing occupational licenses shall be subject to review every five years to determine whether the licensing requirement continues to be justified. Licensing requirements shall be narrowly tailored to address specific identified harms and shall not exceed what is necessary to protect the public. Universal recognition of occupational licenses issued by other states is hereby established.

title: Taxpayer Bill of Rights Act
text: State revenues shall not increase by a percentage greater than the sum of the rate of inflation plus the percentage change in state population without voter approval. Any revenue collected in excess of this limit shall be refunded to taxpayers in proportion to their tax liability. No new tax or tax rate increase shall take effect without approval by a two-thirds supermajority of both chambers of the legislature or approval by a majority of voters in a statewide referendum. The legislature shall not circumvent these limitations through fees, assessments, or other non-tax revenue mechanisms.

title: Next Generation Charter Schools Act
text: Charter schools are hereby authorized as public schools of choice that operate independently of traditional district governance while remaining accountable for student academic outcomes. Charter schools shall receive per-pupil funding equal to one hundred percent of the district per-pupil allocation for each enrolled student. Districts shall provide equitable access to facilities for charter schools. Charter school authorizers shall be independent of local school districts to prevent conflicts of interest. Successful charter schools shall be permitted to expand enrollment and replicate their model without requiring new authorization.

title: Broadband Deployment and Competition Act
text: No municipality, county, or other political subdivision shall provide retail telecommunications services, cable services, or internet access services in competition with private providers. Government-owned broadband networks are hereby prohibited except in areas where no private provider offers service meeting minimum speed standards. Any existing municipal broadband network shall be offered for sale to private providers before any expansion of service area or infrastructure. State and local governments shall streamline permitting processes for private broadband deployment and limit right-of-way fees to cost recovery only.

title: Welfare Drug Testing Act
text: All applicants for public assistance benefits shall submit to drug testing as a condition of eligibility. Any applicant who tests positive for a controlled substance shall be ineligible to receive benefits for a period of one year from the date of the positive test. Children of ineligible applicants shall continue to receive benefits through a designated protective payee. Applicants who test positive may regain eligibility by completing a substance abuse treatment program. The cost of drug testing shall be reimbursed by applicants who test positive for controlled substances.
